a_issues: Mostly off-target and contaminated with 2026 material. It includes a 2026 CDC summary, 2026 blog posts, and a UK 2026 monitoring page. One CIDRAP result is about 2023 cases, not 2025. Some snippets also introduce later totals (18 cases in 2025, 9 deaths) without epidemiological detail or primary sourcing in the snippet.
b_issues: Also incomplete for the specific question. It does contain the key WHO DON575 result, but the snippet is misleading/truncated: it says Cambodia has reported 27 confirmed human infections including 12 fatalities, which is cumulative since re-emergence/over a longer period, not the 2025 fatal-case count the question asks about. Other snippets are partial and late-year summaries (17 cases, at least 8 deaths by 21 Oct 2025) without the province-by-province/exposure details. It also includes some off-target 2026 material.
reasoning: Neither set, from the snippets alone, clearly provides the epidemiological details needed to answer 'Cambodia H5N1 human fatal cases 2025 affected provinces exposure source epidemiological report' correctly. A has one useful 2025 summary (11 cases, 6 deaths through July) but lacks provinces/exposure specifics and is heavily diluted by irrelevant 2026 and even 2023 results. B has the more authoritative WHO source, but its snippet is too compressed and potentially confusing, mixing cumulative figures rather than clearly stating the 2025 fatal cases and their provinces/exposures. Since both are missing the core province/exposure breakdown and both contain misleading or off-target snippets, neither better enables a correct answer from the returned content alone.
